server.h(3) Library Functions Manual server.h(3)

NAME

server.h

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct acds_client_data_t
Per-client connection data. struct migration_context_t
In-memory host migration context. struct acds_server_t
Discovery server state.

Functions


asciichat_error_t acds_server_init (acds_server_t *server, const acds_config_t *config)
Initialize discovery server. asciichat_error_t acds_server_run (acds_server_t *server)
Run discovery server main loop. void acds_server_shutdown (acds_server_t *server)
Shutdown discovery server. void * acds_client_handler (void *arg)
Per-client connection handler (thread entry point).

Function Documentation

void * acds_client_handler (void * arg)

Per-client connection handler (thread entry point). Processes ACIP packets from a connected client. Handles crypto handshake, then dispatches packets to session/signaling handlers.

Parameters

arg Pointer to client connection context

Returns

NULL (thread exit value)

Definition at line 832 of file src/discovery-service/server.c.

832                                      {
833   tcp_client_context_t *ctx = (tcp_client_context_t *)arg;
834   if (!ctx) {
835     log_error("Client handler: NULL context");
836     return NULL;
837   }
838 
839   acds_server_t *server = (acds_server_t *)ctx->user_data;
840   socket_t client_socket = ctx->client_socket;
841 
842   // Get client IP for logging
843   char client_ip[INET6_ADDRSTRLEN] = {0};
844   tcp_client_context_get_ip(ctx, client_ip, sizeof(client_ip));
845 
846   log_info("Client handler started for %s", client_ip);
847 
848   // Register client in TCP server registry with allocated client data
849   acds_client_data_t *client_data = SAFE_MALLOC(sizeof(acds_client_data_t), acds_client_data_t *);
850   if (!client_data) {
851     tcp_server_reject_client(client_socket, "Failed to allocate client data");
852     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
853     return NULL;
854   }
855   memset(client_data, 0, sizeof(*client_data));
856   client_data->joined_session = false;
857   client_data->handshake_complete = false;
858 
859   // Initialize crypto handshake context with client IP name
860   char crypto_name[96];
861   SAFE_SNPRINTF(crypto_name, sizeof(crypto_name), "crypto_discovery_client_%s", client_ip);
862   asciichat_error_t handshake_result = crypto_handshake_init(crypto_name, &client_data->handshake_ctx, true);
863   if (handshake_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
864     log_error("Failed to initialize crypto handshake for client %s", client_ip);
865     SAFE_FREE(client_data);
866     tcp_server_reject_client(client_socket, "Failed to initialize crypto handshake");
867     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
868     return NULL;
869   }
870 
871   // Set server identity keys for handshake
872   client_data->handshake_ctx.server_public_key.type = KEY_TYPE_ED25519;
873   client_data->handshake_ctx.server_private_key.type = KEY_TYPE_ED25519;
874   memcpy(client_data->handshake_ctx.server_public_key.key, server->identity_public, 32);
875   memcpy(client_data->handshake_ctx.server_private_key.key.ed25519, server->identity_secret, 64);
876 
877   if (tcp_server_add_client(&server->tcp_server, client_socket, client_data) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
878     SAFE_FREE(client_data);
879     tcp_server_reject_client(client_socket, "Failed to register client in registry");
880     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
881     return NULL;
882   }
883 
884   log_debug("Client %s registered (socket=%d, total=%zu)", client_ip, client_socket,
885             tcp_server_get_client_count(&server->tcp_server));
886 
887   // Perform crypto handshake (three-step process)
888   log_debug("Performing crypto handshake with client %s", client_ip);
889 
890   // Step 1: Start handshake (send server key, receive client key)
891   handshake_result = crypto_handshake_server_start_socket(&client_data->handshake_ctx, client_socket);
892   if (handshake_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
893     log_warn("Crypto handshake start failed for client %s", client_ip);
894     tcp_server_remove_client(&server->tcp_server, client_socket);
895     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
896     return NULL;
897   }
898 
899   // Step 2: Authentication challenge (if required)
900   handshake_result = crypto_handshake_server_auth_challenge_socket(&client_data->handshake_ctx, client_socket);
901   if (handshake_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
902     log_warn("Crypto handshake auth challenge failed for client %s", client_ip);
903     tcp_server_remove_client(&server->tcp_server, client_socket);
904     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
905     return NULL;
906   }
907 
908   // Step 3: Complete handshake (verify and finalize) - skip if already complete
909   if (client_data->handshake_ctx.state != CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_READY) {
910     handshake_result = crypto_handshake_server_complete_socket(&client_data->handshake_ctx, client_socket);
911     if (handshake_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
912       log_warn("Crypto handshake complete failed for client %s", client_ip);
913       tcp_server_remove_client(&server->tcp_server, client_socket);
914       SAFE_FREE(ctx);
915       return NULL;
916     }
917   } else {
918     log_debug("Crypto handshake already complete from auth_challenge step for client %s", client_ip);
919   }
920 
921   client_data->handshake_complete = true;
922   log_info("Crypto handshake complete for client %s", client_ip);
923 
924   // Main packet processing loop
925   while (atomic_load_bool(&server->tcp_server.running)) {
926     packet_type_t packet_type;
927     void *payload = NULL;
928     size_t payload_size = 0;
929 
930     // Receive packet (blocking with system timeout)
931     int result = receive_packet(client_socket, &packet_type, &payload, &payload_size);
932     if (result < 0) {
933       // Check error context to distinguish timeout from actual disconnect
934       asciichat_error_context_t err_ctx;
935       bool has_context = HAS_ERRNO(&err_ctx);
936 
937       // Check if this is a timeout (non-fatal) or actual disconnect (fatal)
938       asciichat_error_t error = GET_ERRNO();
939       if (error == ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT ||
940           (error == ERROR_NETWORK && has_context && strstr(err_ctx.context_message, "timed out") != NULL)) {
941         // Timeout waiting for next packet - this is normal, continue waiting
942         log_debug("Client %s: receive timeout, continuing to wait for packets", client_ip);
943         if (payload) {
944           buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_size);
945         }
946         continue;
947       }
948 
949       // Actual disconnect or fatal error
950       log_info("Client %s disconnected", client_ip);
951       if (payload) {
952         buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_size);
953       }
954       break;
955     }
956 
957     log_debug("Received packet type 0x%02X from %s, length=%zu", packet_type, client_ip, payload_size);
958 
959     // Multi-key session creation protocol: block non-PING/PONG/SESSION_CREATE messages
960     if (client_data->in_multikey_session_create) {
961       bool allowed =
962           (packet_type == PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_SESSION_CREATE || packet_type == PACKET_TYPE_PING || packet_type == PACKET_TYPE_PONG);
963 
964       if (!allowed) {
965         log_warn("Client %s sent packet type 0x%02X during multi-key session creation - only SESSION_CREATE/PING/PONG "
966                  "allowed",
967                  client_ip, packet_type);
968 
969         // Send error response
970         acip_transport_t *error_transport =
971             acip_tcp_transport_create("transport_acds_error_response", client_socket, NULL);
972         if (error_transport) {
973           acip_send_error(error_transport, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
974                           "Only SESSION_CREATE/PING/PONG allowed during multi-key session creation");
975           ACDS_DESTROY_TRANSPORT(error_transport);
976         }
977 
978         // Free payload and continue
979         if (payload) {
980           buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_size);
981         }
982         continue;
983       }
984     }
985 
986     // O(1) ACIP array-based dispatch
987     // Set server context for callbacks
988     acip_acds_callbacks_t callbacks = g_acds_callbacks;
989     callbacks.app_ctx = server;
990 
991     asciichat_error_t dispatch_result =
992         acip_handle_acds_packet(NULL, packet_type, payload, payload_size, client_socket, client_ip, &callbacks);
993 
994     if (dispatch_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
995       log_warn("ACIP handler failed for packet type 0x%02X from %s: %s", packet_type, client_ip,
996                asciichat_error_string(dispatch_result));
997     }
998 
999     // Free payload (allocated by receive_packet via buffer_pool_alloc)
1000     if (payload) {
1001       buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_size);
1002     }
1003   }
1004 
1005   // Cleanup
1006   tcp_server_remove_client(&server->tcp_server, client_socket);
1007   log_debug("Client %s unregistered (total=%zu)", client_ip, tcp_server_get_client_count(&server->tcp_server));
1008 
1009   socket_close(client_socket);
1010   SAFE_FREE(ctx);
1011 
1012   log_info("Client handler finished for %s", client_ip);
1013   return NULL;
1014 }

    acip_tcp_transport_create(), acip_acds_callbacks_t::app_ctx,

    tcp_client_context_t::client_socket,
    asciichat_error_context_t::context_message,
    crypto_handshake_init(), CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_READY,
    crypto_handshake_server_auth_challenge_socket(),
    crypto_handshake_server_complete_socket(),
    crypto_handshake_server_start_socket(),
    private_key_t::ed25519, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    ERROR_NETWORK, ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT, GET_ERRNO,
    acds_client_data_t::handshake_complete,
    acds_client_data_t::handshake_ctx, HAS_ERRNO,
    acds_server_t::identity_public,
    acds_server_t::identity_secret,
    acds_client_data_t::in_multikey_session_create,
    acds_client_data_t::joined_session, private_key_t::key,
    public_key_t::key, KEY_TYPE_ED25519, log_debug,
    log_error, log_info, log_warn,

    PACKET_TYPE_PONG, receive_packet(),
    tcp_server::running, SAFE_FREE, SAFE_MALLOC,
    SAFE_SNPRINTF, crypto_handshake_context_t::server_private_key,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::server_public_key, socket_close(),
    crypto_handshake_context_t::state,
    tcp_client_context_get_ip(), acds_server_t::tcp_server,
    tcp_server_add_client(), tcp_server_get_client_count(),
    tcp_server_reject_client(), tcp_server_remove_client(),
    private_key_t::type, public_key_t::type, and
    tcp_client_context_t::user_data.


Referenced by acds_server_init().






asciichat_error_t acds_server_init (acds_server_t *
  server, const acds_config_t * config)


Initialize discovery server. Loads or generates identity keys,
    opens database, creates session registry, and binds TCP socket.




Parameters


server Server structure to initialize


Config Module Configuration from command-line
  parsing


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code otherwise


Definition at line 189 of file
    src/discovery-service/server.c.


189                                                                                        {
190   if (!server || !config) {
191     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "server or config is NULL");
192   }
193 
194   memset(server, 0, sizeof(*server));
195   memcpy(&server->config, config, sizeof(acds_config_t));
196 
197   // Open database (SQLite as single source of truth)
198   asciichat_error_t result = database_init(config->database_path, &server->db);
199   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
200     return result;
201   }
202 
203   // Initialize rate limiter with SQLite backend
204   server->rate_limiter = rate_limiter_create_sqlite(NULL); // NULL = externally managed DB
205   if (!server->rate_limiter) {
206     database_close(server->db);
207     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create rate limiter");
208   }
209 
210   // Set the database handle for the rate limiter
211   rate_limiter_set_sqlite_db(server->rate_limiter, server->db);
212 
213   // Configure TCP server
214   tcp_server_config_t tcp_config = {
215       .port = config->port,
216       .ipv4_address = (config->address[0] != '\0') ? config->address : NULL,
217       .ipv6_address = (config->address6[0] != '\0') ? config->address6 : NULL,
218       .bind_ipv4 = (config->address[0] != '\0') || (config->address[0] == '\0' && config->address6[0] == '\0'),
219       .bind_ipv6 = (config->address6[0] != '\0') || (config->address[0] == '\0' && config->address6[0] == '\0'),
220       .accept_timeout_sec = 1,
221       .client_handler = acds_client_handler,
222       .user_data = server,
223   };
224 
225   // Initialize TCP server
226   result = tcp_server_init(&server->tcp_server, &tcp_config);
227   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
228     rate_limiter_destroy(server->rate_limiter);
229     database_close(server->db);
230     return result;
231   }
232 
233   // Initialize background worker thread pool
234   atomic_store_bool(&server->shutdown, false);
235   server->worker_pool = thread_pool_create("acds_workers");
236   if (!server->worker_pool) {
237     log_warn("Failed to create worker thread pool");
238     tcp_server_destroy(&server->tcp_server);
239     rate_limiter_destroy(server->rate_limiter);
240     database_close(server->db);
241     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create worker thread pool");
242   }
243 
244   // Spawn cleanup thread in worker pool
245   if (thread_pool_spawn(server->worker_pool, cleanup_thread_func, server, 0, "cleanup") != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
246     log_warn("Failed to spawn cleanup thread (continuing without cleanup)");
247   }
248 
249   log_info("Discovery server initialized successfully");
250   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
251 }

    acds_server_t::db, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, ERROR_MEMORY,

    rate_limiter_destroy(), rate_limiter_set_sqlite_db(),

Referenced by acds_main().






asciichat_error_t acds_server_run (acds_server_t *
  server)


Run discovery server main loop. Accepts client connections and
    spawns handler threads. Blocks until shutdown signal received.




Parameters


server Initialized server structure


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code otherwise


Definition at line 253 of file
    src/discovery-service/server.c.


253                                                          {
254   if (!server) {
255     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "server is NULL");
256   }
257 
258   log_info("Discovery server accepting connections on port %d", server->config.port);
259 
260   // Delegate to TCP server abstraction
261   return tcp_server_run(&server->tcp_server);
262 }






References acds_server_t::config,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, log_info, acds_config_t::port,
    SET_ERRNO, acds_server_t::tcp_server, and
    tcp_server_run().


Referenced by acds_main().






void
  acds_server_shutdown (acds_server_t * server)


Shutdown discovery server. Closes listen socket, stops accepting
    connections, waits for handler threads to exit, closes database, and frees
    resources.




Parameters


server Server structure to clean up


Definition at line 264 of file
    src/discovery-service/server.c.


264                                                  {
265   if (!server) {
266     return;
267   }
268 
269   // Signal shutdown to worker threads
270   atomic_store_bool(&server->shutdown, true);
271 
272   // Shutdown TCP server (closes listen sockets, stops accept loop)
273   tcp_server_destroy(&server->tcp_server);
274 
275   // Wait for all client handler threads to exit
276   size_t remaining_clients;
277   int shutdown_attempts = 0;
278   const int max_shutdown_attempts = 100; // 10 seconds (100 * 100ms)
279 
280   while ((remaining_clients = tcp_server_get_client_count(&server->tcp_server)) > 0 &&
281          shutdown_attempts < max_shutdown_attempts) {
282     log_debug("Waiting for %zu client handler threads to exit (attempt %d/%d)", remaining_clients,
283               shutdown_attempts + 1, max_shutdown_attempts);
284     platform_sleep_ms(100);
285     shutdown_attempts++;
286   }
287 
288   if (remaining_clients > 0) {
289     log_warn("Server shutdown: %zu client handler threads still running after 10 seconds", remaining_clients);
290   } else if (shutdown_attempts > 0) {
291     log_debug("All client handler threads exited gracefully");
292   }
293 
294   // Stop and destroy worker thread pool (cleanup thread, etc.)
295   if (server->worker_pool) {
296     thread_pool_destroy(server->worker_pool);
297     server->worker_pool = NULL;
298     log_debug("Worker thread pool stopped");
299   }
300 
301   // Destroy rate limiter
302   if (server->rate_limiter) {
303     rate_limiter_destroy(server->rate_limiter);
304     server->rate_limiter = NULL;
305   }
306 
307   // Close database
308   if (server->db) {
309     database_close(server->db);
310     server->db = NULL;
311   }
312 
313   log_info("Server shutdown complete");
314 }






References atomic_store_bool(), database_close(),
    acds_server_t::db, log_debug, log_info,
    log_warn, platform_sleep_ms(),
    acds_server_t::rate_limiter, rate_limiter_destroy(),
    acds_server_t::shutdown, acds_server_t::tcp_server,
    tcp_server_destroy(), tcp_server_get_client_count(),
    thread_pool_destroy(), and acds_server_t::worker_pool.
